title: Detection of Mycotoxins with a Rapid Immunoassay
author: Batrinou, Anthimia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This presentation is part of the training material of FunShield4Med 2nd Summer School entitled "Tools & Strategies to Manage the Mycotoxins Menace" held at Agricultural University of Athens on 10-14 June 2024. This presentation introduces Reveal® Q+, a rapid lateral flow immunoassay designed for on-site, quantitative detection of total aflatoxins (B1, B2, G1, G2) in foods like nuts, grains, and spices. With a detection limit as low as 2 ppb, the method offers a fast (6-minute) and user-friendly alternative to lab-based testing. The assay operates on a competitive binding principle, where signal intensity inversely reflects toxin concentration. Step-by-step procedures—from sample extraction to strip reading—are described, and results are interpreted visually or using the AccuScan® Gold reader. It’s a practical solution for monitoring compliance with EU aflatoxin limits.
